subtitle: Absences blocking case 68974493; urgent team KPI presentation due today
context: Julie Hickman from Access Support is on annual leave returning Monday 15 June, blocking case 68974493 Chemistry bulk-delete request which awaits two critical items from your team before Access Group can proceed. James Salas Guillen is absent from 5 June through Thursday 18 June, leaving the DSE system integration with Cardinus and Healthy Working Plus data uploads unmonitored - the automated PeopleXD upload went live 3 June but requires oversight on contractor record creation. Athena Artuso's email on Holiday Records Duty with proposed reports review arrived at 09:43 today unread with attachments requiring your response. Sophie Levy from Cority has waited 2+ months (last contact April) without reply on the Scoping Session update and is actively waiting to hear from you. Sarah Rowles' escalation on case 68351079 regarding HESA Module LEVELS UDF data cleardown arrived Tuesday 9 June at 12:32 unread with attachments still pending action. Your critical deadline is Simon Burford's Team Meeting today at 10:00 requiring KPI and Statistics presentations from each team - your KPI presentation for May 2026 was shared with Michael O'Sullivan yesterday. Open immediately the unread email from Crispin Muncaster at 09:17 today about the College staff in PeopleXD proposal meeting reminder, as he references Simon's materials in readiness for 'later this morning'.

calToday.title: Clinical pay uplift 2026 kick off meeting

prioritiesToday.description: Smart notes in Cority CoreEHS+ (GX2) do not allow edits after save and close. Raised November 2025, dev ref CCC-19664. Cority's official position (confirmed 08 Jun 2026 by Mehran Riaz, support leadership): this is an INTENDED feature — not a bug or missing feature. Cority classify the request to allow post-save edits as an enhancement, and it is NOT on their current roadmap. No timeline will be provided. Beta/early access ('enhanced access support') requires Premium Support, which Oxford does not have. Oxford has no named account manager — we are in a pool. Escalation route: support case owner → support leadership (done). If account/contract items needed, Cority sales team can be asked to reach out. Quality PUG on Tue 16 Jun at 2pm remains the formal pressure point.
actions: [Nov 2025] Ticket 00476764 raised by James — smart notes cannot be edited after save/close.
[Nov–Jun] Multiple 'still working on it' updates from Freeda Franks (support analyst). Dev ref CCC-19664 assigned.
[08 Jun 2026] Kevin escalated formally — requested timeline, clarification on cost comment, named account manager.

[08 Jun 2026] Mehran Riaz (support leadership) responded: feature is INTENDED behaviour, classified as enhancement, NOT on roadmap. No timeline. Premium Support required for beta access (Oxford does not have it). No named account manager — pool model. Sales team available if needed.
[MONITOR] Quality PUG — Tue 16 Jun, 2pm. Raise CCC-19664 formally. Use Mehran's response as evidence that standard escalation is exhausted.
[TODO — before PUG] Prepare talking points: 6+ months open, intentional feature reclassification, no roadmap commitment, no account manager, Premium Support paywall.
[TODO — after PUG] Decide whether to pursue via sales/account route or accept as a known limitation and communicate to stakeholders.

prioritiesToday.text: Chemistry bulk delete — get details from Julie, pass to Reenu
prioritiesToday.description: Bulk deletion of appraisal cycle assignments in PeopleXD for Chemistry department. Appraisal cycle name: '(DO NOT USE) CDR2025Chemistry'. 5,568 staff incorrectly assigned and need to be removed. AG contact: Reenu Delaney (cases 68974493 / 67938206). Payment via flex points (£525, approved by Marie). Julie Hickman has run the affected employee report and will share the export via OneDrive.
actions: [Oct 2025] Case 67938206 raised — Chemistry bulk delete of appraisal assignments.
[Apr 2026] Flex points agreed as payment route — case 68974493 raised.
[02 Jun 2026] Marie handed case to Kevin as lead.
[03 Jun 2026] Reenu Delaney requested appraisal cycle name and affected employee list.
[09 Jun 2026] Email sent to Julie Hickman requesting details.
[09 Jun 2026] Julie confirmed: cycle name '(DO NOT USE) CDR2025Chemistry', 5,568 staff to be removed. Report run by Julie — OneDrive export incoming.
[AWAITING] Julie to share OneDrive export of affected employee list.
[TODO — on receipt] Forward cycle name + employee list export to Reenu Delaney at AG to action the deletion.
[TODO] Reply to Julie confirming details received and next steps.

prioritiesWeek.description: Kevin returned from leave w/c 2 June on a phased return (4hrs/day, full remote). Team needs to be briefed directly on his return and working arrangements. Michael and James are already across the situation. The others — remaining FA team members — have not yet heard from Kevin directly. Wednesday catch-up was the planned slot but needs bringing forward to Tuesday 9 June.
actions: [08 Jun] Identified: team briefing needed — Michael and James already aware, others not yet informed.
[09 Jun 2026] Team briefed at FA Team Catch-up — OH outcome, phased return schedule (4hrs/day, mornings, fully remote), org structure context, and flex points ask all covered.
[09 Jun 2026] KPI data sent to Michael for Wednesday HR Systems team meeting presentation. ✓

prioritiesWeek.text: Web pages and URL linking — job posting updates
prioritiesWeek.description: Internal Mobility Principles page moved to a new OxIntranet URL. Three pages still link to the old location: main ops intranet page, professional services search page, and individual vacancy application pages. New URL confirmed live on 3 June. Old page expected to be retired 23/24 June. Agreed approach: update all links to the new URL, use hyperlinked text (“IM Principles” / “internal mobility principles”) rather than displaying the full long URL, test before making any live changes, and check whether links can open in a new tab. Updates planned for 22 June ahead of old page retirement. Follow-up with Laura scheduled 15–16 June.
actions: [03 Jun 2026] Meeting with Laura Porter — reviewed three pages needing link updates.
[03 Jun 2026] New URL confirmed live later that day: https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/internal-mobility-principles.aspx
[TODO — 15/16 Jun] Reconnect with Laura Porter as planned.
[TODO — 22 Jun] Test new URL, update links on all three identified pages, replace visible long URLs with hyperlinked text, check whether links open in a new tab.
[TODO] Email Laura with updates if changes are made before they next meet.
[MONITOR] Old page retirement — expected 23/24 June.

prioritiesWeek.text: Flex points plan before Marie returns
prioritiesWeek.description: 2,935 flex points remaining with expiry approximately early July 2026. Simon and Marie have already agreed a plan will be ready when Marie returns from leave. Chemistry bulk deletion (£525, AG, approved by Marie) is already committed. Remaining points need to be allocated before expiry — options discussed include payroll training and manager holiday compliance reports. Athena has sent an email with holiday compliance requirements that needs to be passed to Michael to assess against current system capability.
actions: [08 Jun] Simon to send Athena's holiday compliance requirements email — awaiting.
[09 Jun 2026] Team asked for flex point usage ideas by end of week at FA Team Catch-up.
[TODO] Once Athena's email received from Simon, pass to Michael — ask him to pull screenshots confirming system meets holiday provision requirements (holiday calculator evidence included).
[TODO] Talk to Michael about additional flex point ideas — get as many quotes to AG as possible.
[TODO] Build tracking document: three columns — agreed, booked, under investigation — with point values against each.
[AWAITING] Simon to send Athena's email. Marie's return to formally sign off plan.

prioritiesWeek.description: A formal change management record needs to be raised in OSM for the DTP1092 College Staff into PeopleXD project. This was not raised by Simon during Kevin's leave and remains outstanding. Marie Cooksey is the approver — the record won't be formally approved until go-live but needs to exist now for audit and governance purposes.
actions: [08 Jun] Confirmed outstanding — Simon did not raise during Kevin's leave.
[TODO] Raise OSM change management record for DTP1092. Set Marie Cooksey as approver.
[TODO] Record does not need Marie's formal sign-off immediately — just needs to exist before go-live.

prioritiesWeek.text: Set up pay admin buy codes — after Simon's assessment
prioritiesWeek.description: Pay admin buy codes need to be set up in PeopleXD for DTP1092. Blocked pending Simon's assessment of what already exists at level 6 in the system. Simon will create missing reference data (Division 901, subdivision, levels 4 and 5) before Kevin proceeds. Context: during Kevin's leave, two org structure changes were incomplete — full department structures had not been created down to pay admin level. Michael had to create missing codes ad hoc when staff requests couldn't be processed. Root cause is linked to the documentation/process gap (see t006). No action from Kevin until Simon gives the green light.
actions: [08 Jun] Confirmed blocked — waiting for Simon's level 6 assessment.
[09 Jun 2026] Context confirmed at FA Catch-up: ad hoc codes created by Michael during Kevin's leave as workaround. Last two structure changes were incomplete at pay admin level.
[AWAITING] Simon to complete assessment and create missing reference data (Division 901, subdivision, levels 4 and 5).
[TODO — after Simon] Set up pay admin buy codes once Simon confirms ready.

prioritiesWeek.description: The shared drive was moved to SharePoint but guidance documentation was not updated to reflect the new location. Only Kevin and Lee were managing the migration, so other team members did not know where to find documents. The documents exist and are in the right place — they are just not signposted correctly. Simon has raised this and Kevin needs to respond in writing before recommending an alignment meeting to bring the whole team up to speed. Michael also needs to be looped in.
actions: [08 Jun] Issue identified and raised by Simon — shared drive moved to SharePoint, guidance not updated.

[09 Jun 2026] Root cause confirmed at FA Team Catch-up: when content moved from shared drive to SharePoint/Teams, guidance pointing to documents was not updated. Created a single point of failure between Kevin and Lee — others didn't know where to look. Documents exist and are correctly placed; signposting is the issue.
[TODO] Write response to Simon explaining the situation and confirming documents are accessible.
[TODO] Recommend alignment meeting to get whole team up to speed on SharePoint structure.
[TODO] Touch base with Michael separately on this.

prioritiesWeek.description: James Salas is on leave for two weeks (returning 18 June). As part of the handover (5 June), Kevin agreed to monitor the weekly DSC upload on Wednesday 10 June. This involves monitoring the SFTP and running system checks. James will be back in time for the following upload so this is a one-off cover.
actions: [05 Jun] James handover completed — Kevin confirmed as cover for Wednesday 10 June DSC upload.
[TODO — 10 Jun] Monitor SFTP and run system checks for DSC upload. Flag any issues immediately.

prioritiesWeek.text: HWP archived users check — Wednesday 10 June
prioritiesWeek.description: Users not present in PeopleXD are being incorrectly archived in Healthy Working Plus (HWP). This affects contractors and non-PXD users. A manual rule has been created for known cases (e.g. Jill Farmer). The H&S admin team are checking on Wednesday 10 June. Two open HWP tickets are pending discussion with Gail Miller — James has confirmed these can wait until he returns on 18 June.
actions: [05 Jun] James flagged issue in handover — users not in PXD being incorrectly archived in HWP.
[05 Jun] Manual rule created for known cases (e.g. Jill Farmer).
[TODO — 10 Jun] Be available Wednesday — H&S admin team checking. Escalate if anything surfaces.
[PARKED] Two open HWP tickets with Gail Miller — wait for James to return 18 June.

prioritiesWeek.text: DSE data feed issues — post go-live
prioritiesWeek.description: DSE data feed went live on Wednesday 3 June 2026. Three issues emerged immediately after go-live that require resolution. 1. SBS opted out of the main DSE system (they have their own) but were included in the initial feed — Azure integrations team is handling their exclusion. 2. Contractors are missing from PeopleXD and therefore absent from the feed despite having DSE requirements — a manual addition process is needed. 3. A small number of users do not have university email addresses, preventing standard access — James is investigating with the supplier. James is on leave until 18 June. Kevin to monitor and flag any escalations. It was noted as disappointing that the SBS exclusion was a known requirement but was not built into the original feed.
